zh.Saltwood Castle, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特薩爾特武德
zh.of Ulcombe, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特武爾可姆
zh.lifeSummary: 當Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier於大約1120年出生在英國英格蘭肯特薩爾特武德時，他的父親Ralph de St Leger30歲，母親Miss de Crevequer26歲。他和Miss de Crevequer至少有3個兒子。他去世於1175年在英國肯特武爾可姆享年56歲，埋葬在英國肯特武爾可姆。

zh-hans.Saltwood Castle, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特薩爾特武德
zh-hans.of Ulcombe, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特武爾可姆
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier于大约1120年出生在英国英格兰肯特薩爾特武德时，他的父亲Ralph de St Leger30岁，母亲Miss de Crevequer26岁。他和Miss de Crevequer至少有3个儿子。他去世于1175年在英国肯特武爾可姆享年56岁，埋葬在英国肯特武爾可姆。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Leger
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Camille, Lucien, Pierre, Yvon, Antoine, Aurele, Emile, Jacques, Marcel, Zoel, Adelard.

French (Léger) and English: from the ancient Germanic personal name Leudgari, composed of the elements leud ‘people’ and gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’ and Latinized as Leodegar(ius). The name was borne by a 7th-century Christian saint, bishop of Autun, whose fame contributed to the popularity of the name in France. See also Ledger and St. Leger .
French (Léger): nickname from léger ‘light, superficial’.

variants.name: Lege
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alphie, Emile, Gaston, Henri, Leonce, Minus, Numa, Pierre, Rodolph.
German: topographic name meaning ‘swamp’.
Altered form of French Léger (see Leger ).

variants.name: Legere
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Armand, Normand, Aurele, Jacques, Adelard, Alban, Antoine, Desire, Eloi, Monique, Yvon.
French (Légère): nickname from légère, a feminine form of Léger ‘light, superficial’ (see Leger 2).
Altered form of French   Leger   1 or 2.

variants.name: Lougee
meanings: Channel Islands: altered form of Laugee, a variant of French Lauger, itself either a variant of Léger (see Leger ) or a variant, with fused definite article l', of Auger .

History: The first recorded American bearer of this surname was John Lougee from the isle of Jersey. Born Jean Laugee and by trade a knitter, he was aged c. 18 or 19 when he emigrated to the USA in the late 1600s, settling in Exeter, NH, where he became known as John Lougee, marrying Anne, the daughter of Colonel Moses Gilman.

variants.name: Ledger
meanings: English: from a Norman personal name, Leodegar, Old French Legier, of ancient Germanic origin (see Leger 1).
English: metonymic occupational name from Middle English legger ‘one who lays stone, a mason’, a derivative of Old English lecgan ‘to lay’.
Americanized form of French   Leger   1.

variants.name: St. Leger
meanings: French (Saint-Léger): habitational name from any of several places in various parts of France named Saint-Léger (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Leodegar, in French St. Léger, the martyred 7th-century bishop of Autun), or a nickname with the prefix Saint as an (ironic) attachment to the personal name or surname Léger (see Leger 1).

Haitian (also Saint-Léger): from Saint-Léger, a nickname, ornamental name, or personal name composed of the French prefix Saint and the personal name Léger (see Leger 1), or perhaps a habitational name from a place in Haiti called Saint-Léger (compare 1 above).
English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Saint-Léger in La Manche or Saint-Léger-aux-Bois in Seine-Maritime, France (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Chevalier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Pierre, Patrice, Serge, Gabrielle, Jacques, Marcel, Michel, Raoul, Sylvain, Adelore, Antoine.

French: from Old French chevalier ‘knight’ (literally ‘horseman, rider’, from Late Latin caballarius, a derivative of caballus ‘horse’). In the Middle Ages only men of comparative wealth were able to afford the upkeep of a riding horse. It is likely that in the majority of cases the surname was originally a nickname, or an occupational name for a knight's servant, rather than a status name, for most men of the knightly class belonged to noble families which had more specific surnames derived from their estates. This was a name of Norman origin in England but probably did not survive the medieval period, and was later reintroduced by Huguenot refugees.

History: Joseph Chevalier from Dieppe in Seine-Maritime, France, married Françoise-Marthe Barton in Montreal, QC, in 1670. Pierre Chevalier dit Labbé and Duchesne from Pont-l'Abbé-d'Arnoult in Charente-Maritime, France, married Marie-Madeleine Ménard in Montreal, QC, in 1697. — In North America, this surname was originally also a secondary surname or dit (‘also called’) name, borne by Alexis Dulignon dit Chevalier (a descendant of Jean Dulignon from La Rochefoucaud in Charente, France), who married Judith (de) Billy in Louiseville, QC, in 1778. — This surname is listed in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors and also in the similar register of the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Cavalier
meanings: French (southern): variant of   Chevalier   ‘knight, rider’. This surname is also found in Britain, where it is of Huguenot origin.
History: André Cavalier from Bordeaux in Gironde, France, married first Isabelle-Barbe Brousillon in New Orleans, LA, c. 1760, and second Marie-Jeanne Le Cossec/Cosse in New Orleans in 1770.

variants.name: Chevallier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Blanchard, Christophe, Francois.
French: variant of   Chevalier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: Gilbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Old French name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from gisil ‘pledge’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. It was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. This was the name of the founder of the only native British religious order (abolished at the Dissolution of the Monasteries), St Gilbert of Sempringham ( ?1083–1189 ), in whose honour it is still sometimes bestowed, especially among Roman Catholics. It gained a wider currency in the 19th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier was born about 1120, in Saltwood, Kent, England, United Kingdom, his father, Ralph de St Leger, was 30 and his mother, Miss de Crevequer, was 26. He had at least 3 sons with Miss de Crevequer. He died in 1175, in Ulcombe, Kent, England, at the age of 56, and was buried in Ulcombe, Kent, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier est né environ 1120, à Saltwood, Kent, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ralph de St Leger, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Miss de Crevequer, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils avec Miss de Crevequer. Il est décédé en 1175, à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre, à l’âge de 56 ans, et a été enterré à Ulcombe, Kent, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier um 1120 in Saltwood, Kent, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph de St Leger 30 und seine Mutter Miss de Crevequer 26. Er hatte mit Miss de Crevequer mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1175 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Ulcombe, Kent, England und wurde in Ulcombe, Kent, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier è nato il circa 1120 nel Saltwood, Kent, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Ralph de St Leger, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Miss de Crevequer, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli con Miss de Crevequer. Morì nel 1175, nel Ulcombe, Kent, England, all'età di 56 anni, e fu sepolto a Ulcombe, Kent, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Gilbert de St Leger Chevalierが約1120年にイギリスイギリスケントサルトウッドで生まれた時，父Ralph de St Legerは30歳，母Miss de Crevequerは26歳であった。彼はMiss de Crevequerとの間に少なくとも3人の息子をもうけた。彼は1175年にイギリスケントウルコンベで56歳で死去し，イギリスケントウルコンベにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier(이)가 대략 1120년 영국 잉글랜드 켄트 살트우드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph de St Leger(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Miss de Crevequer(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Miss de Crevequer(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1175년 영국 켄트 울코브에서 56세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 켄트 울코브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier nasceu aproximadamente 1120, em Saltwood, Kent, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Ralph de St Leger, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Miss de Crevequer, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos com Miss de Crevequer. Ele faleceu em 1175, em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, com 56 anos, e foi sepultado em Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier родился около 1120 г. в местности салтвуд, кэнт, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Ralph de St Leger было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Miss de Crevequer, 26. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына от Miss de Crevequer. Он умер в 1175 г. в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия в возрасте 56 лет и был похоронен в местности улкомб, кэнт, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Gilbert de St Leger Chevalier nació aproximadamente 1120, en Saltwood, Kent, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Ralph de St Leger, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Miss de Crevequer, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos con Miss de Crevequer. Murió en 1175, en Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra, a la edad de 56 años, y fue sepultado en Ulcombe, Kent, Inglaterra.
